IDirect3DDevice8::PrimeVertexCache

Primes both the pre and post-transformation and lighting vertex caches, allowing the title to control when vertices are ejected from the caches.

HRESULT PrimeVertexCache(
  UINT VertexCount,
  CONST WORD *pIndexData
);

Parameters

VertexCount
[in] The number of vertices with which to prime the pre and post-transformation and lighting vertex cache. The largest value that should typically be used is 16.
pIndexData
[in] Pointer to the index data.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

Remarks

If a vertex is in the post-transformation and lighting cache, then the entire transformation and lighting step of the GPU is skipped. It has already been done.

The pre-transformation and lighting cache is a standard memory cache, similar to a CPU cache except that it only handles reads, not writes. Its 4 KB, 4-way set associative, with 128 32-byte cache lines.

The post-transformation and lighting cache is a 24-entry FIFO, where each entry stores one transformed-and-lit vertex in its entirety, with caching keyed off the index of the vertex. (In truth, the number of entries that are actually available varies dynamically and is considerably less than 24, except under circumstances that are unlikely to occur in real meshes, as discussed below.) Thus, as each new vertex is emitted from the vertex shader, it's placed at the head of the post-transformation and lighting cache, and the oldest entry in the cache is evicted. (It's guaranteed that any vertex coming out of the vertex shader isn't already in the post-transformation and lighting cache, because if it were, there'd be no need to shade it.) Note that any of the entries in the post-transformation and lighting cache can be read from the cache at any time; it's only with respect to eviction that the cache is organized in FIFO order.

Although the post-transformation and lighting cache has 24 entries, it should really be treated as if there are only 16 entries.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.
